architecture_fit.note: Hand-built geometric reader recovers family + params straight off the matrix; minimal, on-task, and honestly avoids the private generators.

baseline_comparison.note: Shows +0.75 lift over the majority-local baseline and renders it as a dashed line; baseline is the provided majority-class strawman rather than an adversarial one.

faithfulness.note: Synthetic method, no model to patch; README argues each rule is the minimal thing whose removal breaks one family but the ablations are described, not run/shown.

operating_range.note: Only evaluated on the canonical 16 (8x8, seed 0, canonical params); code handles larger windows/global_pos=N-1/other grids but none is demonstrated.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is a hand-set geometric circuit with no learning — exactly the hand-built-weights case.

visual_judgement.note: Demo shows matrix heatmap + displacement footprint (the quantity the classifier consumes) + per-family accuracy bar vs baseline — legible and well chosen.

visualisation_rationale.note: 'Why this visualisation' ties the footprint directly to the classifier's input and explains how each family reads off it.

notes: A clean, honest hand-built geometric classifier that recovers all four pattern families perfectly on the canonical batch and is fully hand-set (no peeking at generators). Main weaknesses are operating range — nothing beyond the canonical 16 is tested despite code that generalizes — and faithfulness, where the self-ablation argument is stated but not demonstrated. Strong visualisation and baseline framing keep it just below the good threshold.
